authoritative_sources.title: CCRS Data Model File Specifications Manual
authoritative_sources.publisher: Washington State Liquor and Cannabis Board
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Washington cannabis regulator manual defining CCRS inventory transfer, lab test, plant, product, and sale file dependencies and required identifiers for analytics source lineage.
used_for: required identifiers and dependencies across inventory, transfer, lab, product, and sale records

authoritative_sources.title: WAC 314-55-087 Recordkeeping requirements for cannabis licensees
authoritative_sources.publisher: Washington State Legislature / Washington State Liquor and Cannabis Board
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Washington cannabis rule requiring retained sale, tax, testing, transport, theft, and source documents and defining audit-trail expectations for ADP and POS systems.
used_for: minimum retained evidence for metric support
refusal when source documents or audit trail are incomplete

authoritative_sources.title: WAC 314-55-089 Tax and reporting requirements for cannabis licensees
authoritative_sources.publisher: Washington State Legislature / Washington State Liquor and Cannabis Board
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Washington cannabis reporting rule specifying monthly reporting cadence, no-activity reporting, excise handling, and the requirement that state traceability data stay completely current.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Metric refresh, period-close, and excise-aware reporting review
used_for: lost, destroyed, and excise-sensitive metric boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: 1 CCR 212-3 Colorado Marijuana Rules
authoritative_sources.publisher: Colorado Secretary of State / Marijuana Enforcement Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Colorado rules publication defining daily inventory reconciliation, secondary-system constraints, transparent inventory-tracking-system data entry, and manifest reconciliation requirements for regulated marijuana.
used_for: limits on POS or BI systems relative to the state tracking system
conflict handling for manifest and inventory discrepancies

authoritative_sources.title: Massachusetts Seed-to-Sale Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Massachusetts Commission guidance defining Metrc as the seed-to-sale system of record, license and package semantics, transfer-manifest rules, correction windows, point-of-sale linkage, and testing-transfer boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Metric definition, terminology normalization, and transfer or POS review
used_for: medical-versus-adult-use sales separation and POS handling
timeliness rules for corrections, reconciliation, and transfer-after-testing

authoritative_sources.title: FAQ | Administrative Order No. 5
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Massachusetts Commission guidance for how COAs, remediation or sanitization disclosures, and Metrc access govern what can be safely reported about testing status and product quality.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Testing, COA, and remediation-aware analytics review
used_for: refusal when testing status is unresolved

authoritative_sources.title: Review and Assessment of the Massachusetts Adult and Medical Use Cannabis Industries
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Massachusetts Commission industry report defining public market metrics such as gross sales, price per gram, price per unit, and product-type sales shares used in benchmark and disclosure contexts.
used_for: public metric definitions and benchmark posture
limits on cross-market and cross-category comparisons

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC MD&A final rule is the authoritative public baseline for KPI-style contextualization and caveat discipline when cannabis analytics feeds executive, board, or investor-style narratives.

workflow_stages.name: Jurisdiction, license, and source-hierarchy intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm jurisdiction, license class, medical-versus-adult-use posture, reporting period, system of record, and source freshness before analysis starts.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Seed-to-sale, POS, finance, and lab record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate package, batch, transfer, manifest, sales, adjustment, test-status, COA, and excise-sensitive records against the named systems of record and retained source documents.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and denominator control
workflow_stages.description: Define the requested metric, denominator, exclusions, segmentation, and benchmark logic while preserving cannabis-specific distinctions such as adult-use versus medical, package status, and test or remediation posture.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cross-system reconciliation and confidence scoring
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile seed-to-sale, POS, accounting, lab, and public benchmark records without collapsing conflicts or allowing a secondary system to silently override the state traceability truth set.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Analysis package assembly and disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Assemble the report, metric dictionary, narrative summary, confidence posture, and action thresholds with explicit evidence lineage and audience context, then stop when disclosure, remediation, regulator-reporting, or legal authority would be required.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: jurisdiction, license number, license class, and business-unit records
seed-to-sale package, plant, harvest, production-batch, manifest, transfer, destruction, and inventory-adjustment records
point-of-sale receipts, discounts, returns, delivery records, and medical-versus-adult-use sales segregation records
lab sample records, test status, COAs, remediation or sanitization disclosures, and hold or recall indicators
incident reports, plans of correction, and unresolved exception trackers
metric dictionaries, dashboard definitions, board-deck drafts, and disclosure review notes
public benchmark reports and market metric publications from state cannabis regulators
systems_of_record: state seed-to-sale or inventory tracking system such as CCRS or Metrc
document management or GRC evidence repository
lab information or testing records repository
BI, reporting, or data warehouse environment
licensing portal or regulator correspondence repository

allowed: classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source hierarchy required for the analysis
prepare evidence-backed metric definitions, analytical briefs, caveat notes, reconciliation notes, and threshold summaries without overstating authority
escalate: required jurisdiction, license class, medical-versus-adult-use handling, source records, or authoritative source context is missing and would change the outcome
public source guidance, tenant policy, and operating practice conflict materially about metric logic, reconciliation posture, or disclosure use
the request raises remediation, product-release, regulator-reporting, tax-signoff, legal, or external disclosure questions outside the lane
refuse: publish or imply certainty when source quality, signoff, or metric definitions are unresolved
treat secondary POS, BI, or spreadsheet outputs as sufficient when the state traceability system materially disagrees
approve remediation, release, regulator submissions, or legal conclusions because the trends appear favorable
state unsupported causal, forecast, benchmark, or disclosure claims

domain_failure_modes: Treating cannabis analytics as generic retail or BI work and failing to request jurisdiction, license class, and state system-of-record context.
Collapsing medical and adult-use sales, excise treatment, or license-specific reporting obligations into a single undifferentiated metric.
Letting POS, spreadsheet, or data-warehouse outputs override the state traceability truth set without preserving the conflict.
Publishing trend conclusions while test status, COA access, remediation posture, or hold indicators remain unresolved.
Using public cannabis benchmarks across jurisdictions, product categories, or unit definitions without recording comparability limits.
Turning descriptive cannabis trends into unsupported causal, legal, release, or disclosure claims.
Allowing the analytics lane to widen into regulatory reporting, compliance signoff, remediation approval, tax approval, or controlled-substance interpretation.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports
Scenario proving escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific metric publication and product taxonomy differences
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public benchmark definitions such as price per gram, product category names, and market segmentation vary by state. The package treats cross-jurisdiction comparisons as tenant-scoped and caveat-bound unless the tenant supplies an approved normalization method.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific equivalent-gram, sell-through, and package-status adjustments
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Operators may use internal equivalency tables or exclude display, sample, hold, or waste states from reporting. The lane requires those definitions to be retrieved and recorded explicitly rather than guessed.

notes: Authoritative cannabis analytics records are the tenant-designated state seed-to-sale system, POS and finance systems, lab evidence repositories, and regulator-published benchmark sources constrained by the cited public rules and guidance.

The lane stops before regulator reporting signoff, remediation approval, product release, legal interpretation, tax approval, and formal external disclosure authority even when the analysis is operationally urgent.
